title: State Conference Schedule Released — Check Your Event Time Slots
body: The official 2026 Colorado FBLA State Conference schedule has been released on the Colorado FBLA website. All competitors should log in to check their specific event time slots for April 2–4. Mobile Application Development presentations are scheduled for April 3 beginning at 9:00 AM. Make sure you arrive at least 15 minutes early for your assigned presentation room. Contact your chapter advisor if you have scheduling conflicts.

title: Congratulations to District 12 Qualifiers for State!
body: We are so proud to announce that Cherry Creek District 12 has qualified 47 members across 23 events for the 2026 Colorado FBLA State Conference! This is our best showing in district history. Special shoutouts to our Mobile App Dev team, Website Design competitors, and all our Business Technology qualifiers. Let's bring home some gold from Denver this April! 🏆

title: New FBLA Membership Benefits for 2026 Announced
body: FBLA National has announced an expanded benefits package for the 2026-2027 school year. Highlights include free access to LinkedIn Learning, a new partnership with Google Career Certificates, and expanded scholarship opportunities totaling over $2 million. Members who renew their membership by March 1 are automatically enrolled in all new benefit programs. Check the Resources tab for the full announcement PDF.

title: Mobile Application Development — Presentation Tips from Past Winners
body: Having placed Top 10 at NLC twice in Mobile App Dev, I want to share my biggest presentation tips with everyone going to State. First: know your rubric cold — judges are looking specifically for syntactic AND semantic input validation, MVVM architecture explanation, and direct social media integration. Second: demo your app on a physical device, not a simulator — it looks more professional. Third: prepare for 'why React Native?' — have a clear, confident answer. Good luck to everyone! You've got this. 💪

title: NLC 2026 Atlanta — Early Bird Hotel Registration Opens April 10
body: Mark your calendars: Early Bird hotel registration for the 2026 FBLA National Leadership Conference in Atlanta opens on April 10 — just one week after State! The official FBLA hotel block fills fast, and competitors are REQUIRED to stay within the block. Rooms are around $149/night and must be booked through the official FBLA housing portal. We will share the exact link in #resources-and-study as soon as it goes live.

title: Business Ethics Workshop Recording Now Available in Resources
body: The recording from last week's Business Ethics workshop with guest speaker Dr. Jennifer Walsh from DU's Daniels College of Business is now available in the Resources tab. The session covered ethical decision-making frameworks, whistleblower scenarios, and how to present ethical analysis in competition. Members competing in Business Ethics, Organizational Leadership, or any case study event should watch this before State.

title: Cherry Creek Chapter Wins Community Service Award
body: We are thrilled to announce that Cherry Creek HS District 12 has won the Colorado FBLA Community Service Award for 2026! Our 'Tech for Seniors' project — where members taught basic smartphone skills to residents at Sunrise Senior Living — logged over 400 volunteer hours and impacted 87 seniors. This award reflects the incredible dedication of every member who showed up on Saturday mornings. We are so proud of this chapter. 🎉

title: FBLA Week 2026 — Activities and Theme Days Announced
body: FBLA Week 2026 is coming up April 28 – May 2! This year's theme is 'Lead. Innovate. Succeed.' We have planned Spirit Days, a community business tour, a resume workshop with local HR professionals, and a chapter social at Elitch Gardens. Monday is FBLA Blue & Gold Day, so wear your colors proudly. Full schedule and permission slips are in the Resources tab under Chapter Events.

title: Interview Skills Webinar Recap — Recording Available
body: Last Tuesday's Interview Skills webinar with Colorado FBLA state officer Maya Williamson was excellent. Key takeaways: Use the STAR method for every behavioral question, maintain steady eye contact (75% of the time), and always bring a printed copy of your resume. The recording is now live in the Resources tab under 'Professional Development.' Watch it before State — many competitors have judge Q&A sessions that feel exactly like professional interviews.

title: State Officer Candidate Applications Now Open
body: Applications for the 2026-2027 Colorado FBLA State Officer team are now open! If you're a current 9th-11th grader with a strong FBLA record, we encourage you to apply. The officer team includes President, EVP, Secretary, Treasurer, Reporter, and Parliamentarian. Applications require a resume, two recommendation letters, and a 2-minute recorded introductory video. Deadline is May 1. Application link available in the Resources tab.

title: How to Prepare Your App Demo in 7 Minutes
body: Seven minutes goes faster than you think. Here is the time structure I use: 0:00–0:45 Intro (who you are, what the app does, why it matters). 0:45–1:30 Problem statement (the gap ConnectFBLA fills). 1:30–4:30 Demo walkthrough (show every rubric-scored feature). 4:30–5:30 Architecture & code quality explanation. 5:30–6:15 UX design rationale. 6:15–7:00 Conclusion. Practice this OUT LOUD until you can do it in your sleep. Judges can tell the difference.

title: Colorado FBLA Partners with Local Business Leaders for Mentorship
body: Colorado FBLA has announced a new mentorship partnership with the Denver Metro Chamber of Commerce. Starting this spring, FBLA members can be matched with a professional mentor from over 200 local businesses. Mentors include executives from Lockheed Martin, Charles Schwab, DaVita, and Centura Health. This is an incredible opportunity to build real-world professional connections before heading to college. Sign up in the Colorado FBLA member portal — slots are limited.

title: New Resources Added: Website Design Event Study Guides
body: We just added 6 new resources to the Website Design event section in the Resources tab! These include HTML5/CSS3 best practices, a UX journey mapping guide, SEO and accessibility checklist, and a full 7-minute presentation script template. These were contributed by last year's state finalists. Website Design competitors — please review all six resources before April 2. They are exactly what judges are looking for.

title: Member Spotlight: Vedaant Pareek Advances to State in Mobile App Dev
body: We are proud to spotlight Vedaant Pareek from Cherry Creek HS for advancing to the Colorado State Conference in Mobile Application Development! Vedaant built ConnectFBLA — a full-featured React Native app that reimagines how FBLA members stay connected. The app features a news feed, calendar, resources library, and a complete group chat and direct messaging system. Vedaant placed first in the district preliminary and will present at State on April 3. Best of luck, Vedaant! 🚀

title: Reminder: NLC Registration Deadline April 22 — Don't Miss It
body: Important reminder for all State competitors: If you advance from State to Nationals, you will need to register for NLC by April 22, 2026. This is a HARD deadline — no exceptions. NLC registration requires your school advisor to complete the online form and submit payment through the FBLA National portal. Start the conversation with your advisor NOW so you are not scrambling if you advance. See the FBLA NLC 2026 resource in the Resources tab for full details.
